content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>按照你建議的都修改</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-07</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
看完了。這份建議的原始情境跟 bridge 不同——它是「一次性強模型 session 立制度給弱模型沿用」的交辦 prompt，而 bridge preamble 是每個 session 重複載入的 runtime prompt，而且你們剛把固定成本砍了 30%，每加一行都要付長期 token 成本。所以我的評估是：**吸收原則、不搬條文**，真正值得進 preamble 的只有少數幾條，其他更適合落在 config / 文件層。

## 值得吸收的（按價值排序）

**1. 任務交辦三要素 + 回報合約 → 加進 delegate notes（最高價值）**

建議檔第 C 條：「交辦要有目標與動機、驗收條件、回報格式；subagent 只回結論與檔案:行號，長產物存檔後傳路徑」。目前 `buildRelayDelegateNote` / `buildParallelDelegateNote`（memory.ts:564,625）只講 token 格式規則（不可含 `>>`、要有 tool call），**完全沒教 agent 怎麼寫一個好的 goal prompt**。委派品質差的根因通常就是 goal 只有目標沒有驗收條件，對方做完你沒法驗。加 2-3 行：

```
- goal 應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式（結論+檔案:行號，長產物存檔回傳路徑）
```

成本約 100 chars，直接提升 RELAY_DELEGATE / PARALLEL_DELEGATE 的產出可用性。

**2. 隨做隨寫（增量存檔）→ 強化 RESTART note 或 disciplines**

建議檔第 3 條「每完成一項立刻存檔，隨時可能中斷」。目前 `[WS]` working-state 協定只在 agent **主動** `<<RESTART>>` 前觸發——但 crash / force-kill / idle sweep 走 `onUnexpectedExit`，agent 來不及存，只剩 session archive 的 5 則回覆摘要。加一行「多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，不要等 RESTART 才存」能讓非計畫性中斷的恢復品質明顯變好。這是 bridge 真實存在的缺口。

**3. 驗證不自驗的最小版：寫檔後 read-back**

建議檔收尾第 2 條。你們已經有 `ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail` skill 和 repo CLAUDE.md 的「strReplace 改完立刻 grep/read 驗證」，但那是 per-repo 的；preamble 的 Agent disciplines 沒有這條，換到其他工作目錄就沒了。在 disciplines 加一行「file edit 後必 read-back 驗證，不信 tool 的 success 回報」很便宜，且對 Kiro 的 silent-fail 是實證踩過的坑。

**4. 「讀者是弱模型」的寫法審計（原則，不是新增內容）**

建議檔開頭這句對 bridge 特別適用——preamble 同一份文字要餵 Kiro / Codex / Claude 三種 adapter，能力參差。可以用這個標準回頭掃一遍 disciplines：每條是否都有「觸發條件 + 動作」而非抽象要求。現況大部分已達標（REFLEXION、ASK BUTTONS 都有明確 trigger），但這是好的持續性 lint 標準，可以寫進 `POLICIES/` 當 preamble 修改的 checklist 項。

## 已經覆蓋、不用重複吸收的

- **常載檔=索引、內容按需引用**（規則 6）：這正是 bridge 現行設計——facts tail + topic shard 索引、wiki 只放標題、indexes 只放 slug。方向被驗證了，但沒有新東西。
- **重試上限 / 換路不重試**：REFLEXION discipline 已有「同法失敗 ≥2 次停手換路，絕不第三次」。
- **不編造、查不到就標註**：MEMORY HONESTY discipline + repo CLAUDE.md Section 11 事實主張閘門已覆蓋。
- **指揮官不下場**：CONTEXT BUDGET discipline 已有「研究型任務 >2 fetch 或 >10 tool calls → 委派」。

## 不適合搬的

- 「這輩子唯一一次」「額度當預算」的框架是一次性交辦情境，bridge 是常駐 runtime，照搬會誤導。
- 「CLAUDE.md ≤150 行」的具體數字是對方環境的值；bridge 的對應物是 preamble char budget，你們已經用 `preambleBreakdown` 在管。若要吸收，比較好的形式是在 smoke script 加一個 preamble 總長上限的斷言（超標 fail），而不是把數字寫進 prompt。

## 建議落點總結

| 建議 | 落點 | 成本 |
|------|------|------|
| 交辦三要素 + 回報合約 | `buildRelayDelegateNote` / `buildParallelDelegateNote` | ~100-150 chars |
| 增量 [WS] 存檔 | RESTART note 或 disciplines 加一行 | ~80 chars |
| 寫檔後 read-back | Agent disciplines 加一行 | ~60 chars |
| 弱模型可讀性標準 | `POLICIES/` preamble 修改 checklist，非 preamble 本體 | 0（文件層）|
| Preamble 長度上限 | smoke script 斷言，非 prompt | 0 |

前三項合計約 +300 chars（~75 tokens），相對剛省下的 30% 是可接受的回填。要我動手改的話說一聲——依 frozen-snapshot policy，改 `buildPreamble*` 前我會先過 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md` 的 checklist。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

建議檔第 3 條「每完成一項立刻存檔，隨時可能中斷」。目前 `[WS]` working-state 協定只在 agent **主動** `<<RESTART>>` 前觸發——但 crash / force-kill / idle sweep 走 `onUnexpectedExit`，agent 來不及存，只剩 session archive 的 5 則回覆摘要。加一行「多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，不要等 RESTART 才存」能讓非計畫性中斷的恢復品質明顯變好。這是 bridge 真實存在的缺口。

**3. 驗證不自驗的最小版：寫檔後 read-back**

建議檔收尾第 2 條。你們已經有 `ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail` skill 和 repo CLAUDE.md 的「strReplace 改完立刻 grep/read 驗證」，但那是 per-repo 的；preamble 的 Agent disciplines 沒有這條，換到其他工作目錄就沒了。在 disciplines 加一行「file edit 後必 read-back 驗證，不信 tool 的 success 回報」很便宜，且對 Kiro 的 silent-fail 是實證踩過的坑。

**4. 「讀者是弱模型」的寫法審計（原則，不是新增內容）**

建議檔開頭這句對 bridge 特別適用——preamble 同一份文字要餵 Kiro / Codex / Claude 三種 adapter，能力參差。可以用這個標準回頭掃一遍 disciplines：每條是否都有「觸發條件 + 動作」而非抽象要求。現況大部分已達標（REFLEXION、ASK BUTTONS 都有明確 trigger），但這是好的持續性 lint 標準，可以寫進 `POLICIES/` 當 preamble 修改的 checklist 項。

## 已經覆蓋、不用重複吸收的

- **常載檔=索引、內容按需引用**（規則 6）：這正是 bridge 現行設計——facts tail + topic shard 索引、wiki 只放標題、indexes 只放 slug。方向被驗證了，但沒有新東西。
- **重試上限 / 換路不重試**：REFLEXION discipline 已有「同法失敗 ≥2 次停手換路，絕不第三次」。
- **不編造、查不到就標註**：MEMORY HONESTY discipline + repo CLAUDE.md Section 11 事實主張閘門已覆蓋。
- **指揮官不下場**：CONTEXT BUDGET discipline 已有「研究型任務 >2 fetch 或 >10 tool calls → 委派」。

## 不適合搬的

- 「這輩子唯一一次」「額度當預算」的框架是一次性交辦情境，bridge 是常駐 runtime，照搬會誤導。
- 「CLAUDE.md ≤150 行」的具體數字是對方環境的值；bridge 的對應物是 preamble char budget，你們已經用 `preambleBreakdown` 在管。若要吸收，比較好的形式是在 smoke script 加一個 preamble 總長上限的斷言（超標 fail），而不是把數字寫進 prompt。

## 建議落點總結

| 建議 | 落點 | 成本 |
|------|------|------|
| 交辦三要素 + 回報合約 | `buildRelayDelegateNote` / `buildParallelDelegateNote` | ~100-150 chars |
| 增量 [WS] 存檔 | RESTART note 或 disciplines 加一行 | ~80 chars |
| 寫檔後 read-back | Agent disciplines 加一行 | ~60 chars |
| 弱模型可讀性標準 | `POLICIES/` preamble 修改 checklist，非 preamble 本體 | 0（文件層）|
| Preamble 長度上限 | smoke script 斷言，非 prompt | 0 |

前三項合計約 +300 chars（~75 tokens），相對剛省下的 30% 是可接受的回填。要我動手改的話說一聲——依 frozen-snapshot policy，改 `buildPreamble*` 前我會先過 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md` 的 checklist。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\POLICIES\\\\preamble-frozen-snapshot.md\",\"content\":\"# Preamble Frozen-Snapshot Policy\\n\\n**Audience**: anyone editing `src/sessionManager.ts`, `src/memory.ts`,\\nor anything in the path that feeds Kiro / Codex / Claude's first prompt.\\n**Status**: enforced invariant, not an aspiration.\\n**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — same rationale, different\\nplumbing.\\n\\n## The invariant\\n\\nThe bridge preamble (tool notes + env block + memory block) is built\\n**once per ACP session** at session creation time. It is **never** rebuilt\\nwhile a session is live.\\n\\nConcretely:\\n\\n- `sessionManager.create()` calls `buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId)`\\n  exactly once and freezes the result into `session.memoryPreamble` +\\n  `session.preambleBreakdown`.\\n- `sessionManager.get()` returns the existing `ChatSession` object\\n  untouched when the session is still open (`!client.isClosed`). No\\n  re-read of the facts file, no re-measurement.\\n- `session.preambleInjected` is a latch: preamble text is included in\\n  the **first** `client.prompt()` call for that session and never again.\\n  Subsequent user turns send only the user text.\\n\\nThe preamble is only rebuilt when a **new session** is created, which\\nhappens in these four paths and no others:\\n\\n1. `get()` sees `existing.client.isClosed` → falls through to `create()`.\\n2. Session is idle past `config.sessionIdleMs` → `sweepIdle()` drops it\\n   and the next `get()` rebuilds.\\n3. Agent process crashes / exits unexpectedly → `exit` handler deletes\\n   the session from the map; next `get()` rebuilds.\\n4. User issues `/restart` or `/drop`, or the bridge shuts down and\\n   restarts.\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nThe Kiro / Codex / Claude ACP agents cache their system-prompt prefix\\naggressively at the LLM provider layer (Anthropic / OpenAI prompt\\ncaching, 5-minute TTL on most tiers). If the bridge were to rebuild\\npreamble mid-session and re-send it, **every user turn would invalidate\\nthe prefix cache**. For a 3–5 KB preamble that is re-hit 50 times in a\\nlong session, that's the difference between `~0 input-token cost for\\ncached content` and `full input-token cost × 50`. It can turn a cheap\\nchat into an expensive one by 1–2 orders of magnitude on the cacheable\\nportion.\\n\\nThis is the same reason Hermes Agent's docs hammer the point (see their\\n`prompt-assembly.md` and the `# Prompt Caching Must Not Break` policy\\nin their AGENTS.md): **the only time we alter the cached prefix is when\\nwe're already throwing the cache away** (i.e. starting a new session).\\n\\n## Forbidden mid-session operations\\n\\nDo **not** add code that:\\n\\n- Calls `buildPreamble*()` after `create()` has returned.\\n- Mutates `session.memoryPreamble` / `session.preambleBreakdown` in place.\\n- Re-reads `facts-<userId>.md` on every turn to \\\"keep memory fresh\\\".\\n- Reloads `topics.json` / `notebooklm-routing.json` and re-injects them\\n  into the live preamble.\\n- Hot-reloads SKILL.md contents into the system prompt.\\n- Resets `session.preambleInjected = false` to \\\"re-inject\\\" newer context.\\n\\nIf you think you need any of the above, you almost certainly want a new\\nsession instead — drop the old one (`session.client.close()` and\\n`this.sessions.delete(chatId)`) so the next `get()` rebuilds. That's\\nthe right way to invalidate cache.\\n\\n## Allowed mid-session operations\\n\\nThese are fine because they don't touch the cached prefix:\\n\\n- Writing to disk: `remember()` / `forget()` / scheduler state / queued\\n  messages / history → all legitimate, all mid-session-safe.\\n- Updating ephemeral state on the `ChatSession` object that is not part\\n  of the preamble: `buffer`, `toolLog`, `charsIn/Out`, `usage`,\\n  `stderrTail`, `inflight`, `lastUsed`.\\n- Appending content to the **current user turn** via tool outputs or\\n  MCP responses. Those are post-prefix, not cached.\\n- Scheduler-triggered commands (`/memorytoskill`, `/topicreview`, etc.)\\n  — these open their own ephemeral ACP client and never touch the\\n  live user session.\\n\\n## Practical consequence for memory writes\\n\\nWhen the agent (or the bridge, on the user's behalf) calls `remember()`\\nmid-session:\\n\\n1. The fact is appended to `facts-<userId>.md` **immediately**.\\n2. The topic shard at `facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md` is updated\\n   **immediately**.\\n3. The live preamble is **not** updated.\\n4. The agent will see the new fact next time the session is rebuilt —\\n   i.e. after `/restart`, or after `sessionIdleMs` elapses, or after a\\n   manual `/drop`.\\n\\nThis is **by design**, not a bug. Document any user-facing \\\"I'll\\nremember that for next time\\\" phrasing accordingly.\\n\\n## If you really need mid-session awareness\\n\\nThe correct escape hatches, in order of preference:\\n\\n1. **Tool response**: let the agent call an MCP tool (e.g. `list_facts`)\\n   to query the live state. Tool responses are not cached and always\\n   see disk.\\n2. **Next-turn user message**: queue a synthetic next-turn message like\\n   \\\"Note: just added to memory: …\\\" — this appends after the cached\\n   prefix and is fine.\\n3. **End the session**: if the change is significant enough to need to\\n   be in the prefix, `/restart` is cheap and correct.\\n\\nWhat you **must not** do is rebuild the preamble silently. That breaks\\nthe invariant this document exists to protect.\\n\\n## Change-this-policy checklist\\n\\nBefore any PR that touches `buildPreamble*`, `memoryPreamble`,\\n`preambleInjected`, or the `SessionManager.create()` codepath:\\n\\n- [ ] Confirm the change does not cause preamble to be re-emitted on any\\n  turn other than the first of a new session.\\n- [ ] Grep for any new caller of `buildPreamble*()` outside\\n  `sessionManager.ts` — if there is one, it's suspect.\\n- [ ] If the change rebuilds sessions more often (e.g. lowers\\n  `sessionIdleMs`, adds a reason for reset), explicitly justify it\\n  against the prompt-cache cost.\\n- [ ] If you remove or weaken `preambleInjected` latch semantics,\\n  replace this document with an up-to-date one (or delete it and amend\\n  `AGENTS.md`).\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- `src/sessionManager.ts` — `create()` is the canonical build point.\\n- `src/memory.ts` — `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` is the only preamble\\n  builder.\\n- `.kiro/steering/windows-shell-safety.md` — sibling steering doc.\\n- Hermes Agent docs, `developer-guide/prompt-assembly.md` — external\\n  reference for the same pattern in a different codebase.\\n\",\"numLines\":143,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":143}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

```xml
<summary>
  <request>User requested to apply all suggested modifications to bridge preamble based on prior recommendations</request>
  <investigated>Claude evaluated a comprehensive set of prompt improvement suggestions against the current bridge preamble system, analyzing fit for a runtime prompt vs one-time delegation context. Assessed each suggestion's value relative to the recent 30% preamble cost reduction and ongoing token budget constraints.</investigated>
  <learned>Four suggestions have high value: (1) Task delegation three-elements (goal+motivation, acceptance criteria, report format) currently missing from buildRelayDelegateNote/buildParallelDelegateNote (~100 chars); (2) Incremental [WS] checkpoint saves for crash recovery, as current working-state protocol only triggers on deliberate RESTART (~80 chars); (3) Write-then-read-back verification in disciplines to catch silent edit failures (~60 chars); (4) "Weak model readability" standard as a review checklist for future preamble changes (documentation layer, 0 cost). Many other suggestions already covered: REFLEXION has retry limits, MEMORY HONESTY covers no-fabrication, CONTEXT BUDGET has delegation thresholds. Some suggestions don't fit: "one-time" framing inapplicable to runtime prompts, specific line limits belong in smoke tests not prompts.</learned>
  <completed>Analysis and prioritization completed with cost estimates. Identified total addition of ~300 chars (~75 tokens) across three high-value improvements, representing acceptable backfill against recent 30% savings.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting confirmation to implement the three preamble additions (delegate notes enhancement, incremental checkpointing, read-back verification). Will follow POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md checklist before modifying buildPreamble* functions.</next_steps>
  <notes>This evaluation demonstrates disciplined cost/benefit analysis for prompt engineering: rejecting additions that duplicate existing coverage or don't fit the runtime context, while identifying genuine gaps (delegation quality, crash recovery, edit verification) that solve real observed failure modes like Kiro's silent strReplace failures and unexpected exit data loss.</notes>
</summary>
```

That's\\nthe right way to invalidate cache.\\n\\n## Allowed mid-session operations\\n\\nThese are fine because they don't touch the cached prefix:\\n\\n- Writing to disk: `remember()` / `forget()` / scheduler state / queued\\n  messages / history → all legitimate, all mid-session-safe.\\n- Updating ephemeral state on the `ChatSession` object that is not part\\n  of the preamble: `buffer`, `toolLog`, `charsIn/Out`, `usage`,\\n  `stderrTail`, `inflight`, `lastUsed`.\\n- Appending content to the **current user turn** via tool outputs or\\n  MCP responses. Those are post-prefix, not cached.\\n- Scheduler-triggered commands (`/memorytoskill`, `/topicreview`, etc.)\\n  — these open their own ephemeral ACP client and never touch the\\n  live user session.\\n\\n## Practical consequence for memory writes\\n\\nWhen the agent (or the bridge, on the user's behalf) calls `remember()`\\nmid-session:\\n\\n1. The fact is appended to `facts-<userId>.md` **immediately**.\\n2. The topic shard at `facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md` is updated\\n   **immediately**.\\n3. The live preamble is **not** updated.\\n4. The agent will see the new fact next time the session is rebuilt —\\n   i.e. after `/restart`, or after `sessionIdleMs` elapses, or after a\\n   manual `/drop`.\\n\\nThis is **by design**, not a bug. Document any user-facing \\\"I'll\\nremember that for next time\\\" phrasing accordingly.\\n\\n## If you really need mid-session awareness\\n\\nThe correct escape hatches, in order of preference:\\n\\n1. **Tool response**: let the agent call an MCP tool (e.g. `list_facts`)\\n   to query the live state. Tool responses are not cached and always\\n   see disk.\\n2. **Next-turn user message**: queue a synthetic next-turn message like\\n   \\\"Note: just added to memory: …\\\" — this appends after the cached\\n   prefix and is fine.\\n3. **End the session**: if the change is significant enough to need to\\n   be in the prefix, `/restart` is cheap and correct.\\n\\nWhat you **must not** do is rebuild the preamble silently. That breaks\\nthe invariant this document exists to protect.\\n\\n## Change-this-policy checklist\\n\\nBefore any PR that touches `buildPreamble*`, `memoryPreamble`,\\n`preambleInjected`, or the `SessionManager.create()` codepath:\\n\\n- [ ] Confirm the change does not cause preamble to be re-emitted on any\\n  turn other than the first of a new session.\\n- [ ] Grep for any new caller of `buildPreamble*()` outside\\n  `sessionManager.ts` — if there is one, it's suspect.\\n- [ ] If the change rebuilds sessions more often (e.g. lowers\\n  `sessionIdleMs`, adds a reason for reset), explicitly justify it\\n  against the prompt-cache cost.\\n- [ ] If you remove or weaken `preambleInjected` latch semantics,\\n  replace this document with an up-to-date one (or delete it and amend\\n  `AGENTS.md`).\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- `src/sessionManager.ts` — `create()` is the canonical build point.\\n- `src/memory.ts` — `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` is the only preamble\\n  builder.\\n- `.kiro/steering/windows-shell-safety.md` — sibling steering doc.\\n- Hermes Agent docs, `developer-guide/prompt-assembly.md` — external\\n  reference for the same pattern in a different codebase.\\n\",\"numLines\":143,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":143}}"</outcome>

`POLICIES/`，真正的內容只有這\\n一份。\\n\\n## 新增 policy 的流程\\n\\n1. 在這個資料夾建立 `<policy-name>.md`。\\n2. 在本 README 的索引表補一列。\\n3. 如果新 policy 對某個 agent 特別重要，可以在 `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`\\n   的 steering stub 裡額外帶一行「Key policy: see `POLICIES/<file>.md`」。\\n4. `.kiro/steering/` 不用動 — 那邊的 stub 統一指向整個 `POLICIES/` 目錄。\\n\\n## 改 policy 的流程\\n\\n直接改這個資料夾的檔案。三邊 agent 下一次 session 就會讀到更新後的版本\\n（`AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / Kiro stub 都是 file reference，不是副本）。\\n\",\"numLines\":42,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":42}}"</outcome>

  <subtitle>Single source of truth architecture prevents drift across Kiro, Codex, Claude agent configurations</subtitle>

Since bridge supports Kiro CLI, Codex, and Claude Agent simultaneously (commit 519eb6a), each with different steering file conventions, the architecture uses stub references from each agent's entry point (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .kiro/steering/) that point to the canonical POLICIES/ directory. This ensures that policy changes automatically propagate to all agents on their next session without manual synchronization. The README documents four existing policies covering session caching, Windows tool safety, project context conventions, and documentation obligations. Understanding this structure is relevant for the planned "weak model readability" checklist, which should become a new policy document rather than being embedded directly in the preamble.</narrative>

lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter((l) => l.trim().length > 0);\\n  const total = lines.length;\\n\\n  // tail=0 → preserve old behaviour (inject everything, no index).\\n  if (opts.tail === 0 || total <= opts.tail) {\\n    return (\\n      \\\"[Long-term memory about this user — use when relevant, don't mention unless asked]\\\\n\\\" +\\n      lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n Convenience so callers don't re-measure. */\\n  total: number;\\n  /** sendFileNote + askNote + scheduleNote combined. */\\n  toolNotes: number;\\n  /** Environment block (MEMORY_DIR / SKILL_DIR / AGENT_CONFIG_DIR / USER_ID). */\\n  envBlock: number;\\n  /** Long-term memory block (recent facts + topic index). 0 if no facts. */\\n  memoryBlock: number;\\n  /** Daily log block (most recent dailylog). 0 if none. */\\n  dailylogBlock: number;\\n  /** Wiki index block (page titles from wiki/index.md). 0 if no wiki. */\\n  wikiBlock: number;\\n  /** Document tree indexes listing. 0 if no indexes. */\\n  indexBlock: number;\\n  /** Available agent-developed scripts (tool-* in scripts/). 0 if none. */\\n  scriptsBlock: number;\\n  /** User profile block (structured user identity/preferences). 0 if none. */\\n  userProfileBlock: number;\\n  /** Stripped tail facts included in preamble — for dedup in memory-recall. */\\n  tailFacts: Set<string>;\\n}\\n\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Like buildPreamble but also returns per-section char lengths so the\\n * bridge can display where context tokens are going. Prefer this in\\n * new code; buildPreamble is kept for callers that only want the text.\\n */\\nexport function buildPreambleWithBreakdown(\\n  facts: string,\\n  userId?: number,\\n  opts?: { tailOverride?: number; compressed?: string | null }\\n): PreambleBreakdown {\\n  const {\\n    sendFileNote,\\n    askNote,\\n    scheduleNote,\\n    skillUsedNote,\\n    goalDoneNote,\\n    restartNote,\\n    stickerNote,\\n    wikiQueryNote,\\n    skillProposeNote,\\n    continueNote,\\n    relayDelegateNote,\\n    specialistProxyNote,\\n    parallelDelegateNote,\\n    rtkNote,\\n    envBlock,\\n    memoryBlock,\\n    dailylogBlock,\\n    wikiBlock,\\n    indexBlock,\\n    scriptsBlock,\\n    userProfileBlock,\\n  } = buildPreambleSections(facts, userId, opts?.tailOverride, opts?.compressed);\\n  const text =\\n    sendFileNote +\\n    askNote +\\n    scheduleNote +\\n    skillUsedNote +\\n    goalDoneNote +\\n    restartNote +\\n    stickerNote +\\n    wikiQueryNote +\\n    skillProposeNote +\\n    continueNote +\\n    relayDelegateNote +\\n    specialistProxyNote +\\n    parallelDelegateNote +\\n    rtkNote +\\n    envBlock +\\n    userProfileBlock +\\n    memoryBlock +\\n    dailylogBlock +\\n    wikiBlock +\\n    indexBlock +\\n    scriptsBlock;\\n  // Build a Set of stripped tail facts for dedup in memory-recall.\\n  const tailCount = opts?.tailOverride ?? config.memoryPreambleTail;\\n  const tailFacts = new Set<string>();\\n  if (facts) {\\n    const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter((l) => l.trim().length > 0);\\n    const recent = lines.slice(-tailCount);\\n    for (const line of recent) {\\n      tailFacts.add(line.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\").trim());\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    text,\\n    total: text.length,\\n    toolNotes:\\n      sendFileNote.length +\\n      askNote.length +\\n      scheduleNote.length +\\n      skillUsedNote.length +\\n      goalDoneNote.length +\\n      restartNote.length +\\n      stickerNote.length +\\n      wikiQueryNote.length +\\n      skillProposeNote.length +\\n      continueNote.length +\\n      relayDelegateNote.length +\\n      specialistProxyNote.length,\\n    envBlock: envBlock.length,\\n    memoryBlock: memoryBlock.length,\\n    dailylogBlock: dailylogBlock.length,\\n    wikiBlock: wikiBlock.length,\\n    indexBlock: indexBlock.length,\\n    scriptsBlock: scriptsBlock.length,\\n    userProfileBlock: userProfileBlock.length,\\n    tailFacts,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nexport function buildPreamble(facts: string, userId?: number): string {\\n  return buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId).text;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Minimal preamble for local models (Ollama). Keeps total under ~2000 tokens\\n * so small models don't choke on an oversized system prompt.\\n * Includes: identity, recent facts, memory tool hints.\\n */\\nexport function buildLocalPreamble(facts: string, userId?: number): PreambleBreakdown {\\n  const lines = facts ? facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter((l) => l.trim().length > 0) : [];\\n  const tail = Math.min(5, lines.length);\\n  const recent = lines.slice(-tail);\\n  const recentContent = recent.map((l) => l.trim()).join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n\\n  const toolGuide =\\n    \\\"[MCP Tools — call these via tool_call]\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"• remember({ text }) — store a fact to long-term memory. Use for user preferences, decisions, lessons.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"• list_facts({ query, tail? }) — retrieve facts. query=topic name or substring. tail caps results (default 20).\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"• forget({ query, confirm? }) — delete facts by substring match. let block =\\n    `[Long-term memory about this user — recent ${recent.length} of ${total} facts, use when relevant]\\\\n` +\\n    recentContent +\\n    \\\"\\\\n[End memory]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n\\n  if content.thinking:
The user is editing src/memory.ts to add the incremental checkpoint guidance to the RESTART note. This is the first of the three planned improvements - adding guidance for agents to save [WS] completed: checkpoints during multi-step tasks rather than waiting for restart, to handle non-planned interruptions (crash / idle / force-kill).

This is a concrete implementation of one of the prioritized improvements from the earlier analysis.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Incremental checkpoint guidance added to RESTART tool note</title>
  <subtitle>Agents now instructed to save [WS] completed checkpoints at milestones, not just at restart</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added 2 lines (~160 chars) to restartNote in src/memory.ts buildPreambleSections() function instructing incremental checkpoint saves</fact>
    <fact>New guidance instructs agents to remember() a [WS] completed: fact after each milestone in multi-step tasks</fact>
    <fact>Rationale provided: session may be interrupted non-planned (crash / idle / force-kill) before agent can execute deliberate RESTART</fact>
    <fact>Placement is immediately after [WS] next_action field documentation and before Guardrail line in RESTART tool note</fact>
    <fact>This addresses current limitation where working-state protocol only triggers on deliberate <<RESTART>>, not on onUnexpectedExit paths</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The first of three prioritized preamble improvements has been implemented. The RESTART tool note in buildPreambleSections() now includes explicit guidance for incremental checkpoint saves during multi-step tasks. Previously, the working-state protocol only captured state when an agent deliberately invoked <<RESTART>>; if the session crashed, was force-killed, or swept due to idle timeout, the agent's progress since the last fact save would be lost except for the 5-message archive summary. The new guidance instructs agents to proactively save [WS] completed: facts after each milestone completion via remember(), ensuring that non-planned interruptions still preserve meaningful progress state. This 160-character addition directly addresses a real failure mode in the bridge's crash recovery path and fits within the planned ~300-char total budget for all three improvements.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/memory.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

 return \\\"\\\";\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Scan indexes/ directory and list available document tree indexes.\\n */\\nfunction buildIndexBlock(): string {\\n  const indexDir = +\\n        \\\"Pending files at: ${MEMORY_DIR}/indexes/.pending/<slug>.md\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End pending ingests]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    return block;\\n  } catch {\\n    return const toolFiles = readdirSync(scriptsDir).filter(f => f.startsWith(\\\"tool-\\\"));\\n    if (toolFiles.length === 0) return \\\"\\\";\\n    const entries: string[] = [];\\n    for (const file of  );\\n  } catch { return \\\"\\\"; }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Reads ${MEMORY_DIR}/user-profile.md and wraps it as a preamble section.\\n * This gives the agent a structured snapshot of the user's return (\\n      \\\"[User profile — structured identity and preferences]\\\\n\\\" +\\n      content + \\\"\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"[End user profile]\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    );\\n  } catch {\\n    return Env override: AGENT_CONFIG_DIR.\\n */\\nexport function detectAgentConfigDir(): string {\\n  const override = process.env.AGENT_CONFIG_DIR;\\n  if (override && override.trim() !== \\\"\\\") return path.resolve(override);\\n  const home = process.env.USERPROFILE || process.env.HOME || \\\"\\\";\\n  if (!home) return \\\"(unknown — set $AGENT_CONFIG_DIR to override)\\\";\\n  // Heuristic: the agent command name hints which CLI we're paired with.\\n  const agent = (config.agent?.command ?? \\\"\\\").toLowerCase();\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"codex\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".codex\\\");\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"claude\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".claude\\\");\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"kiro\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".kiro\\\");\\n  // Fallback: try .kiro then .codex\\n  const kiroDir = path.join(home, \\\".kiro\\\");\\n  const codexDir = path.join(home, \\\".codex\\\");\\n  if (existsSync(kiroDir)) return kiroDir;\\n  if (existsSync(codexDir)) return codexDir;\\n  return kiroDir; // best guess\\n}\\n\\nexport function detectSkillDir(agentConfigDir?: string): string {\\n  // Prefer an explicit override so the user can point us at any directory.\\n  const override = process.env.SKILL_DIR;\\n  if (override && override.trim() !== \\\"\\\") return path.resolve(override);\\n  // Otherwise SKILL_DIR is just <agentConfigDir>/skills — keeps the two paths\\n  // aligned so users only need to override AGENT_CONFIG_DIR in unusual setups.\\n  const base = agentConfigDir ?? task 改同一個檔案\\\\n\\\";\",\"originalFile\":\"import { mkdir, readFile, appendFile, writeFile } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport { existsSync, readFileSync, readdirSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config, resolveBridgeConfigPath } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\nimport { classifyFact, topicKeywordHint } from \\\"./facts-store.js\\\";\\nimport { compressFactsForPreamble } from \\\"./local-llm.js\\\";\\nimport { listPendingIngests } from \\\"./artifact.js\\\";\\n\\nfunction factsPath(userId: number): string {\\n  return path.join(config.memoryDir, `facts-${userId}.md`);\\n}\\n\\nexport async function loadFacts(userId: number): Promise<string> {\\n  try {\\n    return (await readFile(factsPath(userId), \\\"utf8\\\")).trim();\\n  } catch {\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nexport async function appendFact(userId: number, line: string): Promise<void> {\\n  await mkdir(config.memoryDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const text = line.trim();\\n  const formatted = formatFactLine(text);\\n  // Guard: if the existing file doesn't end with a newline, prepend one so\\n  // we never glue two facts together on the same line.\\n  let prefix = \\\"\\\";\\n  try {\\n    const existing = await readFile(factsPath(userId), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    if (existing.length > 0 && !existing.endsWith(\\\"\\\\n\\\")) prefix = \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n  } catch {\\n    /* file doesn't exist yet — nothing to guard */\\n  }\\n  await appendFile(factsPath(userId), prefix + formatted, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  // Mirror to per-topic shard. Failures here are warned but not rethrown\\n  // because the master log is authoritative.\\n  appendFactShardOnly({\\n    userId,\\n    formattedLine: formatted,\\n    rawText: text,\\n  });\\n}\\n\\n/** Strip leading \\\"- [f_xxxxxx] [timestamp] \\\" or \\\"- [timestamp] \\\" bullet. */\\nfunction stripFactPrefix(line: string): string {\\n  return line.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*(?:\\\\[f_[0-9a-f]{6}\\\\]\\\\s*)?\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\").trim();\\n}\\n\\n/** List existing facts as plain strings (timestamp/bullet stripped). */\\nexport async function listFacts(userId: number): Promise<string[]> {\\n  const raw = await loadFacts(userId);\\n  if (!raw) return [];\\n  return raw\\n    .split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n    .map(stripFactPrefix)\\n    .filter((s) => s.length > 0);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Append facts, skipping ones that duplicate existing lines. Comparison is\\n * per-line after normalising (strip bullet/timestamp, lowercase, collapse\\n * whitespace) — avoids the earlier substring-match footgun where any new\\n * fact sharing a phrase with an older one was silently dropped.\\n */\\nexport async function appendFactsDedup(userId: number, lines: string[]): Promise<number> {\\n  const norm = (s: string) => stripFactPrefix(s).toLowerCase().replace(/\\\\s+/g, \\\" \\\").trim();\\n  const existing = new Set((await listFacts(userId)).map(norm));\\n  let added = 0;\\n  for (const raw of lines) {\\n    const line = stripFactPrefix(raw);\\n    if (!line) continue;\\n    const key = norm(line);\\n    if (existing.has(key)) continue;\\n    await appendFact(userId, line);\\n    existing.add(key);\\n    added++;\\n  }\\n  return added;\\n}\\n\\n// Topic classification rules live in ./topic-rules.ts so mcp-memory can\\n// share the same list and expand topic-name queries into keyword OR match.\\n\\ninterface MemoryBlockOptions {\\n  tail: number; // 0 = inject everything\\n  topics: boolean;\\n  userId?: number; // used to read shard directory for the topic index\\n  /** Pre-compressed recent facts (from local LLM). If set, replaces raw recent lines. */\\n  compressed?: string | null;\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildMemoryBlock(facts: string, opts: MemoryBlockOptions): string {\\n  if (!facts) return \\\"\\\";\\n  const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter((l) => l.trim().length > 0);\\n  const total = lines.length;\\n\\n  // tail=0 → preserve old behaviour (inject everything, no index).\\n  if (opts.tail === 0 || total <= opts.tail) {\\n    return (\\n      \\\"[Long-term memory about this user — use when relevant, don't mention unless asked]\\\\n\\\" +\\n      lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n      \\\"\\\\n[End memory]\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  const recent = lines.slice(-opts.tail);\\n  const older = lines.slice(0, -opts.tail);\\n\\n  // Split recent facts into two tracks: user preferences vs agent notes.\\n  const userPrefLines: string[] = [];\\n  const agentNoteLines: string[] = [];\\n  for (const line of recent) {\\n    const raw = line.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*(?:\\\\[f_[0-9a-f]{6}\\\\]\\\\s*)?\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\");\\n    const topic = classifyFact(raw);\\n    if (topic === \\\"user-preference\\\") {\\n      userPrefLines.push(line);\\n    } else {\\n      agentNoteLines.push(line);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  let recentContent: string;\\n  if (opts.compressed) {\\n    // Compressed mode doesn't split (LLM already summarized)\\n    recentContent = opts.compressed;\\n  } else if (userPrefLines.length > 0) {\\n    recentContent =\\n      \\\"## About this user\\\\n\\\" +\\n      userPrefLines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n      \\\"\\\\n## Agent notes\\\\n\\\" +\\n      agentNoteLines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    recentContent = recent.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n  }\\n\\n  let block =\\n    `[Long-term memory about this user — recent ${recent.length} of ${total} facts, use when relevant]\\\\n` +\\n    recentContent +\\n    \\\"\\\\n[End memory]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n\\n  if (opts.topics && older.length > 0) {\\n    // Preferred path: list the user's shard directory and use each\\n    // file's line count as the topic count. This lets the preamble\\n    // survive topic rule changes (files reflect what's actually on\\n    // disk). Falls back to classify-on-the-fly if the shard dir\\n    // doesn't exist yet (fresh install, pre-rebuild, etc.).\\n    let indexRows: Array<[string, number]> = [];\\n    let source: \\\"shard\\\" | \\\"classify\\\" = \\\"classify\\\";\\n    if (opts.userId !== undefined) {\\n      const shards = listTopicShards(opts.userId);\\n      if (shards.length > 0) {\\n        source = \\\"shard\\\";\\n        indexRows = shards.map((s) => [s.topic, s.count] as [string, number]);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (source === \\\"classify\\\") {\\n      const counts = new Map<string, number>();\\n      for (const line of older) {\\n        const raw = line.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\");\\n        const topic = classifyFact(raw);\\n        counts.set(topic, (counts.get(topic) ?? 0) + 1);\\n      }\\n      indexRows = Array.from(counts.entries()).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);\\n    }\\n\\n    const indexLines = indexRows\\n      .map(([t, n]) => {\\n        const hint = topicKeywordHint(t, 3);\\n        return hint ? `- ${t} (${n}): ${hint}` : `- ${t} (${n})`;\\n      })\\n      .join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n\\n    const headerNote =\\n      source === \\\"shard\\\"\\n        ? `${older.length} older facts (plus any newer ones), sharded at facts_Topic/${opts.userId}/`\\n        : `${older.length} older facts grouped by topic`;\\n\\n    block +=\\n      `[Memory topics — ${headerNote}]\\\\n` +\\n      indexLines +\\n      \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"If the user asks about a topic above and the recent facts don't cover it,\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"call the `list_facts` MCP tool with the topic name — the server reads\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"that shard file directly, e.g. `list_facts({ query: \\\\\\\"bridge-token\\\\\\\" })`.\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"Use query=\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" and tail=0 only if you genuinely need the full set.\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"[End topics]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  // 七項行為紀律的壓縮版（原本各自獨立成塊，約 3.4k chars；壓成單塊保留\\n  // 每條的觸發條件 + 動作，省約一半 preamble 固定成本）。\\n  block +=\\n    \\\"[Agent disciplines]\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ \\\\\\\"Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)\\\\\\\".\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. content.thinking:
The user has made another edit to the memory.ts file, this time modifying the buildRelayDelegateNote() function. They added a line about the "three elements" that a goal should contain: goal+motivation, acceptance criteria, and report format (only return conclusions+file:line, save long outputs to file and return path).

This is the second of three planned improvements - adding task delegation quality guidance to the delegate note builders. The addition is approximately 80 chars in Chinese, adding the three-elements requirement to the format rules section of the RELAY_DELEGATE tool note.

This change improves delegation quality by explicitly teaching agents how to write better goal prompts when delegating tasks to other agents.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Task delegation three-elements added to RELAY_DELEGATE tool note</title>
  <subtitle>Goal prompts now required to include objective+motivation, acceptance criteria, and report format</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added delegation quality guidance (~80 chars) to buildRelayDelegateNote() format rules section in src/memory.ts</fact>
    <fact>New rule: goal 應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式（只回結論+檔案:行號，長產物存檔回傳路徑）</fact>
    <fact>Three elements are objective+motivation, acceptance criteria, and report format (conclusions+file:line references only, long artifacts saved to file with path returned)</fact>
    <fact>Placement in format rules section immediately after goal/prompt character restrictions and before result-waiting guidance</fact>
    <fact>Addresses root cause where delegate quality suffers when goal contains objective without acceptance criteria, making verification impossible</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The second of three prioritized preamble improvements has been implemented. The RELAY_DELEGATE tool note now explicitly teaches agents how to write effective delegation prompts by requiring three elements: (1) objective with motivation for context, (2) acceptance criteria so the delegator can verify completion, and (3) report format specifying that responses should contain only conclusions and file:line references, with long outputs saved to files and referenced by path. This addresses the observed problem where poor delegation quality stems from goal prompts containing only objectives without verifiable success criteria, leaving the delegating agent unable to validate the work. The ~80-character addition fits within the planned budget and directly improves the utility of RELAY_DELEGATE calls by establishing a contract between delegator and delegatee about what constitutes done and how to communicate results efficiently.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>

    <file>src/memory.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

chars, ${raw.chunk_count} chunks)`\\n        );\\n      } catch { /* skip */ }\\n    }\\n    if (entries.length === 0) return \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    let block =\\n      \\\"[Document indexes — use for auto-navigation, or read ${MEMORY_DIR}/indexes/<slug>.json manually\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"To ingest new doc: MUST run `py scripts/prep-ingest.py <file> <slug>` first (copies to indexes/sources/), then ingest_prepare\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"[End indexes]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n\\n    // Pending ingests from researcher artifacts\\n    const pending = listPendingIngests();\\n    if (pending.length > 0) {\\n      block +=\\n        \\\"[Pending ingests — researcher artifacts awaiting tree index build]\\\\n\\\" +\\n        pending.map((p) => `- ${p.slug}: ${p.title} (call ingest_prepare with source=\\\"${p.source}\\\")`).join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n        \\\"\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"Action: for each pending, call ingest_prepare(source=file_path) then ingest_save to build tree.\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"Pending files at: ${MEMORY_DIR}/indexes/.pending/<slug>.md\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End pending ingests]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    return block;\\n  } catch {\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildScriptsBlock(): string {\\n  const scriptsDir = path.join(config.sessionCwd, \\\"scripts\\\");\\n  if (!existsSync(scriptsDir)) return \\\"\\\";\\n  try {\\n    const toolFiles = readdirSync(scriptsDir).filter(f => f.startsWith(\\\"tool-\\\"));\\n    if (toolFiles.length === 0) return \\\"\\\";\\n    const entries: string[] = [];\\n    for (const file of toolFiles) {\\n      try {\\n        const content = readFileSync(path.join(scriptsDir, file), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n        const lines = content.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).slice(0, 5);\\n        const docLine = lines.find(l => /^(\\\\/\\\\/|#)\\\\s*TOOL:/.test(l));\\n        const desc = docLine\\n          ? docLine.replace(/^(\\\\/\\\\/|#)\\\\s*TOOL:\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\").trim()\\n          : \\\"(no docstring)\\\";\\n        entries.push(`- ${file}: ${desc}`);\\n      } catch { entries.push(`- ${file}: (unreadable)`); }\\n    }\\n    return (\\n      \\\"[Available scripts — agent-developed tools in scripts/]\\\\n\\\" +\\n      entries.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") + \\\"\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"Run with: node scripts/<file> or python scripts/<file>\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"[End scripts]\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    );\\n  } catch { return \\\"\\\"; }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Reads ${MEMORY_DIR}/user-profile.md and wraps it as a preamble section.\\n * This gives the agent a structured snapshot of the user's return (\\n      \\\"[User profile — structured identity and preferences]\\\\n\\\" +\\n      content + \\\"\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"[End user profile]\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    );\\n  } catch {\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildEnvironmentBlock(userId?: number): string {\\n  const memoryDir = config.memoryDir;\\n  const agentConfigDir = detectAgentConfigDir();\\n  const skillDir = detectSkillDir(agentConfigDir);\\n  const uid = userId !== undefined ? String(userId) : \\\"(not set — session has no authenticated user yet)\\\";\\n  return (\\n    \\\"[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    `- MEMORY_DIR=${memoryDir}\\\\n` +\\n    `- SKILL_DIR=${skillDir}\\\\n` +\\n    `- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=${agentConfigDir}\\\\n` +\\n    `- USER_ID=${uid}\\\\n` +\\n    \\\"Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"[End environment]\\\\n\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"[Project context — AI.md layered loading]\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"[End project context]\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Detect the agent's config home directory (e.g. ~/.kiro, ~/.codex, ~/.claude).\\n * This is the parent of SKILL_DIR and also hosts agent-wide artefacts like\\n * notebooklm-routing.json or agents/*.json. Env override: AGENT_CONFIG_DIR.\\n */\\nexport function detectAgentConfigDir(): string {\\n  const override = process.env.AGENT_CONFIG_DIR;\\n  if (override && override.trim() !== \\\"\\\") return path.resolve(override);\\n  const home = process.env.USERPROFILE || process.env.HOME || \\\"\\\";\\n  if (!home) return \\\"(unknown — set $AGENT_CONFIG_DIR to override)\\\";\\n  // Heuristic: the agent command name hints which CLI we're paired with.\\n  const agent = (config.agent?.command ?? \\\"\\\").toLowerCase();\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"codex\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".codex\\\");\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"claude\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".claude\\\");\\n  if (agent.includes(\\\"kiro\\\")) return path.join(home, \\\".kiro\\\");\\n  // Fallback: try .kiro then .codex\\n  const kiroDir = path.join(home, \\\".kiro\\\");\\n  const codexDir = path.join(home, \\\".codex\\\");\\n  if (existsSync(kiroDir)) return kiroDir;\\n  if (existsSync(codexDir)) return codexDir;\\n  return kiroDir; // best guess\\n}\\n\\nexport function detectSkillDir(agentConfigDir?: string): string {\\n  // Prefer an explicit override so the user can point us at any directory.\\n  const override = process.env.SKILL_DIR;\\n  if (override && override.trim() !== \\\"\\\") return path.resolve(override);\\n  // Otherwise SKILL_DIR is just <agentConfigDir>/skills — keeps the two paths\\n  // aligned so users only need to override AGENT_CONFIG_DIR in unusual setups.\\n  const base = agentConfigDir ?? Never retry a 3rd time.\\\\n\\\" +\",\"+    \\\"READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.\\\\n\\\" +\",\"+    \\\"Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).\\\\n\\\" +\",\"     \\\"CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.\\\\n\\\" +\",\"     \\\"Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. - "增量存檔" (incremental checkpoint) - FOUND
- "三要素" (three elements) - FOUND  
- "READ-BACK" - not in fixed (may be facts-gated)

The reason READ-BACK isn't in the fixed-cost preamble is because it was added to `buildMemoryBlock()`, which is only included when there are facts. The fixed-cost preamble is built with an empty string `buildPreamble('', 0)`, so there are no facts and thus the memory block (which includes the Agent disciplines) isn't included.

This shows the fixed-cost preamble with real environment but empty facts. Let me analyze:

- toolNotes: 2863 chars (includes all the <<TOOL>> notes like RESTART, RELAY_DELEGATE, PARALLEL_DELEGATE, etc.)
- envBlock: 1342 chars (environment paths and project context)
- core (toolNotes + envBlock): 4205 chars
- total with empty facts: 7175 chars

This is consistent with the earlier 7167 measurement (slight difference likely due to environment-specific paths or wiki/index blocks). The user mentioned that the recent 30% preamble reduction was significant, and now they've added back ~420 chars. Let me see what that means percentage-wise:

If we assume the core was previously around 6900 chars (30% less than, say, ~9900), now it's 7175, so they added back about 275 chars of the 3000 they saved. That's less than 10% of the savings, which seems reasonable.

The key insight here is that the "core" fixed-cost sections (tool notes + env) are 4205 chars, and the rest (7175 - 4205 = 2970) is wiki + other fixed blocks. The memory block with facts adds more on top.

content.thinking:
This is a significant addition to the smoke test. The session is adding a fixed-cost ceiling assertion to prevent preamble bloat. Let me break down what this does:

1. Defines FIXED_CORE_CEILING = 6000 chars
2. Measures toolNotes + envBlock (the fixed-cost core that doesn't grow with facts/wiki)
3. Current measurement is ~4205 chars, so the ceiling gives ~40% headroom
4. If the core exceeds 6000 chars, the test fails with exit code 1
5. This acts as a "tripwire" to prevent unintentional preamble inflation

The comment explains the rationale: these sections are fixed-cost (don't grow with user data), and the 6000 char ceiling is based on 2026-07 measurements of ~4205 chars with ~40% headroom to account for relay/specialist notes that may appear in production environments. If someone adds content that exceeds this, they need to consciously raise the ceiling with an explanation in the commit message.

This is a good practice for maintaining the preamble budget discipline that was mentioned earlier in the conversation about the 30% reduction from compressing disciplines.

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Fixed-cost preamble ceiling assertion added to smoke test</title>
  <subtitle>6000-char tripwire prevents unintentional bloat in toolNotes + envBlock core sections</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added fixed-cost ceiling assertion to scripts/check-preamble.mjs enforcing 6000-char limit on toolNotes + envBlock</fact>
    <fact>Ceiling based on 2026-07 measurement of 4205 chars with ~40% headroom for relay/specialist notes in production</fact>
    <fact>Test fails with exit code 1 if fixedCore exceeds FIXED_CORE_CEILING, forcing conscious review of additions</fact>
    <fact>Prevents preamble inflation by requiring explicit ceiling adjustment with commit explanation when legitimate growth needed</fact>
    <fact>Current fixed core (4205 chars including three new improvements) is 70% of ceiling, leaving 1795 chars headroom</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The preamble smoke test now includes a fixed-cost ceiling assertion to prevent unintentional preamble bloat. The test measures the fixed-cost core—toolNotes plus envBlock—and fails if it exceeds 6000 characters. This ceiling is calibrated based on the current measurement of 4205 characters with approximately 40% headroom to accommodate relay/specialist notes that may be conditionally rendered in production environments. The tripwire ensures that any future additions to tool notes or environment blocks trigger an explicit review: developers must either compress existing content or consciously raise the ceiling with a commit message explaining the justification. This enforces the preamble budget discipline established during the recent 30% reduction from discipline compression, preventing the savings from being eroded by undisciplined additions. The three improvements just added (~420 chars) fit comfortably within the existing budget, leaving 1795 characters of headroom under the ceiling.</narrative>

Frozen-snapshot checklist（見 sibling policy）——確認沒有新增 mid-session\\n   rebuild 路徑。\\n\"}"</parameters>

content.thinking:
The user has created a new policy document called "preamble-authoring.md" which codifies the "weak model readability" principle that was identified as one of the items to absorb from the original recommendations. This is the fourth item from the original evaluation that was planned to go into documentation layer rather than the preamble itself.

The document establishes content standards for writing preamble text, assuming the weakest model as the target audience. It includes checklists for:
- Trigger condition + action (specific rules, not abstract guidance)
- Numeric limits (concrete numbers, not "適量")
- Negative lists (when NOT to use tools)
- Self-contained (don't reference external docs agents may not have read)
- Verifiable (compliance can be judged from response text)

It also documents the fixed-cost budget ceiling enforcement via the smoke test, and requires three post-modification checks: TypeScript compilation, smoke test with ceiling assertion, and frozen-snapshot checklist.

This is the completion of the planned work - the three preamble improvements have been implemented and validated, the smoke test has been enhanced with a ceiling assertion, and now the authoring policy has been documented for future maintainers.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Preamble authoring policy document created for weak-model readability standard</title>
  <subtitle>New policy codifies content standards: trigger+action, numeric limits, negative lists, self-contained, verifiable</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Created POLICIES/preamble-authoring.md establishing content standards for preamble text modifications</fact>
    <fact>Five-point checklist: trigger condition + action, numeric limits, negative lists, self-contained, verifiable rules</fact>
    <fact>Rationale: single preamble serves Kiro/Codex/Claude with varying model capabilities; write for weakest model where abstract requirements equal unwritten</fact>
    <fact>Documents fixed-cost budget enforcement via FIXED_CORE_CEILING in check-preamble.mjs smoke test</fact>
    <fact>Requires three post-modification checks: TypeScript compilation, smoke test with ceiling assertion, frozen-snapshot checklist</fact>
    <fact>Sibling to preamble-frozen-snapshot.md: frozen-snapshot governs when to build, authoring policy governs how to write content</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The fourth and final element from the original recommendations has been implemented: the weak-model readability standard is now codified as a policy document rather than embedded in the preamble. POLICIES/preamble-authoring.md establishes content standards for anyone modifying preamble text, based on the principle that the same preamble serves three ACP adapters (Kiro/Codex/Claude) with varying capabilities and may be switched to cheaper models via .env. The policy provides a five-point checklist for preamble authors: every rule must have explicit trigger conditions and actions (not abstract guidance), numeric limits must be concrete numbers, tools prone to misuse need negative lists explaining when NOT to use them, content must be self-contained without assuming external context, and rules must be verifiable from response text alone. The document also codifies the fixed-cost budget discipline, requiring authors to compress existing content before adding new material or consciously raise the ceiling with justification. It mandates three post-modification checks: TypeScript compilation, smoke test with ceiling assertion, and frozen-snapshot checklist review. This completes the planned work: three preamble improvements implemented, ceiling enforcement added to smoke test, and authoring standards documented for future maintainers.</narrative>
